Customers currently using the legacy Cash Flow Statement solution should begin evaluating their migration timeline. Review SAP Note 3700711 - Replacement of Existing 'Cash Flow Statements - China' App with 'China Cash Flow Statements' Report in Document and Reporting Compliance for the latest information on the transition roadmap and consult the implementation handbook for technical guidance.

As with any significant system change, adequate planning and testing are essential. The investment in migration will position your organization to benefit from enhanced performance, improved data consistency, and access to advanced reporting capabilities that support evolving business needs.

For implementation support, please refer to the SAP Help Portal or contact your SAP representative.

 

FAQ:

Q1: What is SAP DRC?

A: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance is a unique and competitive statutory reporting framework that helps you manage your regulatory requirements across the globe. Statutory reporting, a component of SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, is a global solution that enables timely creation, generation, and submission of statutory reports to authorities in the correct format. It ensures compliance with complex and dynamic legal requirements. For more details, refer to SAP Help Portal for Statutory Reporting.

Q2: For S/4HANA Public Cloud customers, does the new DRC Cash Flow Statement require activation of new scope items?

A: Yes.

  • With 1J2, you can generate and download the China Cash Flow Statement in both PDF and TXT(CSV) format.
  • With additional 5XU, you can preview and edit (change the figures and regenerate files with full audit trail), perform analytics, trace back to source documents, and extend the existing report using design time.

Customers need to contact your SAP Account Executive if they have any question related to licenses.

Q3: After the legacy solution is retired, how can customers use the new solution to view historical Cash Flow data?

A: After you activate and configure the DRC cashflow report, you can run the Data Migration Tool (for Public Cloud and OP, or transaction code FIRU_CF_OFF_ACC) to display historical data in the new DRC cashflow report.

Note:

  1. We recommend that you do the solution switch and run the migration program during year-end financial closing or at the beginning of the year.
  2. If you purchased 5XU, you can use the drill-down tool in the analytics view for troubleshooting. If the new solution is configured correctly, the data in the new and legacy solutions should be consistent (legacy solutions use similar offsetting account determination configuration and calculation logic).
  3. This tool only performs data migration. It can't resolve the effort required for customers to implement the new DRC cashflow report.

Q4: What existing legal reports have been replaced by SAP DRC statutory reports so far?

A: SAP Note 2480067 - Replacement of Existing Legal Reports with DRC reports provides information about the legacy ABAP legal reports that statutory reports provided with the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance framework are succeeding.

entry.title: Validation Messages: CBS and IBS Configuration
entry.content:
As part of the transition to the new CBS (Contribuição sobre Bens e Serviços) and IBS (Imposto sobre Bens e Serviços) tax structure, SAP has introduced three new validation messages when you receive inbound electronic fiscal documents.

Understanding the New Messages 

The messages address common discrepancies that may arise when processing XML documents under Brazil Tax Reform: 

J1B_NFE660: Expected CBS/IBS tax rate is X but tax rate in XML is Y   

This message alerts users when there's a mismatch between the expected tax rate configured in the system and the rate specified in the incoming XML document. 

J1B_NFE661: CBS/IBS tax not available in XML for purchase order item X   

This validation triggers when the required CBS/IBS tax information is missing from the XML for a specific purchase order item. 

J1B_NFE663: Tax group CBS/IBS from XML not available in the current Tax Code   

This message appears when the tax group referenced in the XML document cannot be found in the system's current tax code configuration. 

You can configure each message to appear as either Warning or Error, depending on your business requirements. For detailed instructions on configuring these messages through transaction OBMSG, please refer to Configuring Messages 660, 661, and 663 in OBMSG and OBA5. 

 

Have you found this blog post useful? If so, choose “Like”, share it with your colleagues, and follow me for similar content.  

You are welcome to leave your questions and read similar blog posts on the  SAP Document and Reporting Compliance SAP Community. Don’t forget to follow the  SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica)  tag and the  SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Community  to keep up with the latest NF-e news.  

Thank you, and until next time,  
Juliana Jarotzky

entry.title: GRC, Trade and Tax in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I'm excited to share the update regarding our latest release - GRC, Trade and Tax in SAP Cloud ERP 2602. This update highlights many significant innovations from SAP Risk and Assurance Management, SAP Watch List Screening, SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, as well as SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance.

For a quick overview of the top release highlights for GRC with 2602, you can watch the video below.

Video 1. Release Highlight Video for GRC, Trade and Tax in SAP Cloud ERP 2602

This blog covers the following innovations:

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

  • AI-assisted trend analysis and summary for reports (beta)
  • Embedded analytics to facilitate VAT adjustments
  • E- verify and Automated Handling of Draft VAT Returns from Authorities
  • Enabling Electronic Invoices Through KSeF 2.0 for Poland​

SAP Risk and Assurance Management

  • Extended analytical and reporting capabilities: new reporting dashboards with SAP Analytics Cloud, enterprise edition

SAP Enterprise Threat Detection

  • Intelligent alert handling and investigation recommendations
  • Management-focused monitoring dashboard

SAP International Trade

  • Export declaration for plants abroad in the context of Registration for Indirect Taxation Abroad (RITA)

​SAP Watchlist Screening

  • Delta Screening​

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

AI-assisted trend analysis and report summary

Tax accountants often spend significant time validating data by comparing current tax figures with previous returns. To accelerate this process, we introduce AI-assisted trend analysis for VAT returns. This feature helps tax experts by automatically uncovering hidden trends, flagging outliers, and detecting root causes for discrepancies. It provides intelligent summaries that can be used to simplify the approval process and support future audits. This innovation allows the team to move from reactive validation to proactive analysis.

Picture 1. AI-assisted trend analysis and report summary

Value Proposition​
  • Reduce the time needed to interpret VAT data across periods, leading to faster reviews, quicker approvals, and shorter reporting cycles.
  • Accuracy is improved and compliance risk is reduced. This helps identify anomalies early, minimizing manual errors and lowering the risk of incorrect filings.
  • Easy to make clear and informative data-driven decisions.
Capabilities
  • The analysis highlights key trends, fluctuations, and irregularities, and flags values that help to identify potential errors or compliance risks. ​
  • Provide concise summary of key VAT insights to support faster reviews, approvals, and decision-making.​

Embedded analytics to facilitate VAT adjustments

During the preparation of statutory reports, tax experts require deep visibility into their data to ensure accuracy. With the 2602 release, the 'Manage Tax Items' activity within SAP Document and Reporting Compliance is enhanced with a powerful ''analyze data" option.

This provides tax experts with additional fields and the flexibility to analyze tax data to identify anomalies. Now you can easily see how invoices are mapped to your VAT returns and pinpoint any documents that might have been excluded, enabling seamless adjustments integrated directly with accounting. To further tailor the solution, you can now use extensibility to plug in your own custom analytics CDS views, allowing you to choose between standard SAP-delivered queries or your own defined views for analysis.

 

Video 2. Embedded analytics to facilitate VAT adjustments

Value Proposition​
  • Advances the VAT review and adjustment by integrating analytics.
  • Improves accuracy and strengthens compliance through data-driven decisions.
  • Reduces manual effort and reliance on offline analysis and external tools​.
  • Enhances analytical transparency and enables faster and more confident decision-making.​
Capabilities ​
  • Embedded analytics within the Manage Tax Items for Legal Reporting application​
  • Direct access to detailed VAT tax line-item records with added data points​
  • Interactive analytical views with charts, filters, and drill-down capabilities​
  • Trend analysis and comparisons across multiple VAT dimensions​
  • Identification of documents for inclusion in or exclusion from VAT returns​
  • Analysis at the point of action without switching applications​
  • Support for efficient VAT reconciliation and compliance preparation​

For more information, see: Manage Tax Items for Legal Reporting | SAP Help Portal

E- verify and Automated Handling of Draft VAT Returns from Authorities

A growing number of tax authorities are providing pre-populated, or draft, tax returns based on real-time transactional data. Our latest update helps you automate the handling of these draft returns, ensuring consistency with your company's books. With this feature, you can pull auto-populated returns from an authority's portal and automatically compare them with the returns prepared in your ERP system to identify any mismatches.

Based on the reconciliation results, you can then process actions such as submitting a delta file, accepting, or rejecting the draft. This streamlines the entire process, from review and correction to the submission of the final return.

 

Video 3. E- verify and Automated Handling of Draft VAT Returns from Authorities

Enabling electronic invoices through KSeF 2.0 for Poland

With the Polish Ministry of Finance enforcing the use of electronic invoices through the KSeF 2.0 platform beginning in February 2026, we are equipping you to meet this mandate head-on. SAP Document and Reporting Compliance now enables you to generate B2B e-invoices in the required XML format from both Finance (FI) and Sales & Distribution (SD), including tax invoices, correction invoices, down-payment invoices, and self-billing invoices. You can submit these invoices interactively to KSeF 2.0, visualize them in a human-readable PDF format with a QR code, and retrieve supplier invoices for automated posting.

Picture 2. Enabling electronic invoices through KSeF 2.0 for Poland

SAP Risk and Assurance Management

Extended analytical and reporting capabilities: new reporting dashboards with SAP Analytics Cloud, enterprise edition

We are introducing the new Risk and Compliance Cockpit, an SAP Analytics Cloud story that gives you an instant, comprehensive overview of your organization's compliance landscape.

This dashboard empowers compliance managers to identify process inefficiencies by visualizing compliance issues organized by type and status, with charts showing trends over time. It makes it easy to compare current performance against historical data, highlight long-term trends, and identify deviations. With insights into average issue resolution times and year-to-year analysis, this single source of truth helps you prioritize critical unresolved issues and improve operational efficiency.

 

Video 4.  Extended analytical and reporting capabilities: new reporting dashboards with SAP Analytics Cloud, enterprise edition

Value Proposition​
  • Get an instant overview of compliance issues, organized by type and status​
  • Improved operational efficiency supported by trend and year-to-year analysis​
  • Charts visualize trends over time​
  • Overview of what to prioritize by highlighting critical unresolved issues​
​Capabilities ​
  • Identify inefficiencies in organizations' processes​
  • Instant overview of compliance issues​
  • Filter business data to examine specific categories of compliance issues​
  • Visual and comparative analysis of data​
  • Compare current performance while highlighting long-term trends and deviations

For more information, see: Risk and Compliance Cockpit

SAP Enterprise Threat Detection

Intelligent alert handling and investigation recommendations

In SAP Enterprise Threat Detection, cloud edition, investigations are the central hub for examining security incidents. This new capability automates and enhances this critical process.

The primary value of this feature is to decrease the manual effort required by SAP monitoring agents and increase their overall efficiency. When an agent prepares an investigation to send to a customer, they are now supported with automatic, case-specific response and mitigation recommendations.

This helps teams by automatically compiling investigation results and formulating suggestions for customers on how to address the alerts. The system intelligently generates these recommendations based on the standard operating procedure of the specific pattern that created the alert, further enhanced by predefined value lists and machine learning functionality. This ensures that the advice provided is both highly relevant and immediately actionable, allowing your security teams to move from detection to resolution faster and more effectively.

Picture 3. Intelligent alert handling and investigation recommendations

For more information, see: Generating Investigation Recommendations

Management-focused monitoring dashboard

We are introducing a powerful new monitoring dashboard within SAP Enterprise Threat Detection, cloud edition, specifically designed for management roles. In today's complex security landscape, having a clear, high-level view of your organization's cyberhealth is not just an advantage; it is a necessity. This new dashboard is for all managers and security leaders who need to quickly understand current security events, alerts, and ongoing investigations to make informed, timely decisions.

It facilitates a deeper understanding of complex security data by offering a comprehensive picture of your system's cyberhealth and protection status. The dashboard also shows the distribution of security events by various criteria, effectively highlighting the most affected systems, patterns, and users to help you focus resources where they are needed most.

To ensure the information is tailored to your specific needs, you can also create your own personalized monitoring pages with a selection of charts that are most relevant to your responsibilities..

Picture 4. Management-focused monitoring dashboard

For more information, see: Using the Monitoring Dashboard

SAP International Trade

Export declaration for plants abroad in the context of Registration for Indirect Taxation Abroad (RITA)

For businesses operating with plants abroad, ensuring the correct export declaration is crucial. In a plant abroad scenario, this feature ensures that the country of the delivering plant, rather than the country of the company code, determines the correct foreign trade organization for the customs declaration. This enhancement improves compliance with international trade regulations for businesses leveraging the "Registration for Indirect Taxation Abroad" (RITA) scope.

Value Proposition
  • Enhance compliance with trade regulations by ensuring the correct foreign trade organization is used for export declarations.
Capabilities 
  • Manage your export declaration in SAP Global Trade Services using the foreign trade organization within the country of the plant abroad.
  • Identify plants abroad on pro forma invoice line items by considering countries where registration for indirect taxation (RITA) is enabled.

In SAP Cloud ERP,  a billing document exists where goods delivery is performed via a foreign plant, for example, the plant country is different from the country of the company code.

Picture 5. Export declaration for plants abroad in the context of Registration for Indirect Taxation Abroad (RITA)

In SAP GTS, Edition for SAP HANA, the FTO of the corresponding export declaration is now determined based on the legal unit corresponding to the foreign plant used in the billing document.

Picture 6. Export declaration for plants abroad in the context of Registration for Indirect Taxation Abroad (RITA)

For more information, see: Enablement of Plants-Abroad for Export Processing and Foreign Plant​

Delta screening with SAP Watch List Screening and manual release of blocked document items

Continuous compliance with sanction party lists is critical. Screening requests sent to SAP Watch List Screening will now remain active for delta screening for up to one month. This means the system automatically performs delta screenings against any changes to watch lists and updates the status of your business partners and documents accordingly, providing automatic coverage without requiring new manual screenings.

Additionally, for situations requiring manual intervention, the "Manage Documents - Trade Compliance" Fiori app now allows authorized users to manually release a trade compliance document item that is blocked. This creates a simple, straightforward, and auditable overrule process to comply with legal requirements while ensuring business continuity.

Picture 7. Delta screening with SAP Watch List Screening and manual release of blocked document items

For more information, see: Integration with SAP Watch List Screening and Delta Screening in SAP Watch List Screening

Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series! 

From January 12-16, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find the recording and presentation for Artificial Intelligence as well as 21 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime.

Find all our assets 

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below: 

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on Hiruni Arunodhi Badulle | LinkedIn.

entry.title: Spain Legal Form 303: Changes for 2026
entry.content:
The Spanish Tax Agency (AEAT) has published important updates to legal form 303 (VAT returns) in Order HAC/27/2026 on  January 26, 2026, that will take effect starting with the February 2026 reporting period. These changes introduce new fields and modify the TXT file format for electronic submissions.

  • December 5, 2025 - Draft law for 303 Adjustments published for public consultation
  • January 26, 2026 - Order HAC/27/2026 officially published in BOE
  • February 11, 2026 - Technical specifications for 01-1T (2026)
  • February 13, 2026 - Technical specifications for 02-12M and 2T-4T (2026)

In this post, the changes in the legal form 303 and the customizing will be explained.

 

Legal form adjustments

New Hydrocarbon sales and Deductions

According to this regulation, the following fields are included in teh VAT return:

  • New identification field: "Sujeto pasivo con derecho a deducir pago a cuenta de entregas de gasolinas, gasóleos y biocarburantes posteriores a la ultimación del régimen de depósito distinto del aduanero" (Tax payer eligible for deductions due to the pre payments for the delivery of gasolines and biodiesel after the extraction from Bonded Deposits).
  • New Tax box 112: "Pago a cuenta de entregas de gasolinas, gasóleos y biocarburantes posteriores a la ultimación del régimen de depósito distinto del aduanero atribuible a la Administración del Estado" (Pre-paid amounts for the delivery of gasolines and biodiesel after the extraction from Bonded Deposits)

 

Use case

The following example illustrates the process:

 

  • December:
    • The company A (importer company) imports 1.000L of fuel into the boded/tax warehouse. This transaction happens in a VAT suspension schema 
  • January
    • The company A sales to Company B (extractor company)  1.000L of fuel (this transaction is treated like an import):
      • Sales price: 1,5€/L
      • Tax base amount = 1.500€
      • VAT = 0€ 
    • Before the extraction. Assumption: company B is not registered as OEA (Operador Económico Autorizado, authorized Economic operator as defined in EU 952/2013) or Trustable Operator ("operador confiable" as defined in Orden HAC/1496/2025😞
      • Settle Import VAT with the Tax Agency (legal form 380): 
        • Import VAT (21%) = 1500€ x 21% =  315€.
      • Pre-payment by Company B to the Tax Agency (Legal form 319):  315€ x 110% = 346,50€.
    • After pre-payment:
      • Company A ensures that Company B either made the pre-payment or is identified  as Trustable Operator/OEA and authorizes the extraction. In case the extraction is authorized without this check, the Company A will be held as solidary responsible for the amount of 110% of the corresponding Import VAT.
      • Company B extracts the fuel from the bonded warehouse. 
        • Purchase price: 1,5€/L
        • Tax base amount = 1.500€
        • Import VAT (21%): 315€.
  • February
    • The company B sales 1000L to Company C (final customer).
      • Sales price: 1,7€/L
      • Tax base amount = 1.700€
      • Output VAT = 1700€ * 21% = 357€.
    • VAT return (303) by Company B
      • Output VAT = 1700€ * 21% = 357€
      • Pre-payment (tax box 112) = 346,50€
      • VAT return result = 357€ - 346,50€ = 10,5€ to pay to the Tax Agency.

 

Document and Reporting Compliance

New report configuration parameter ES_VAT_FUEL_DEDUCTION :

 

 The possible values are:

  • “0” = Not applicable (January and 1T, 2026)
  • “1” = Yes, taxpayer is eligible for deduction  
  • “2” =No, taxpayer is not eligible for deductions (as of February, 2T-4T 2026)

 

The taxbox 112 will be visible directly in DRC preview screen:

In case you need to notify a deduction in this tax box, you need to edit this legal form and change the value in the preview screen manually . You need to make sure that once the tax box is updated:

  • Return type is correct. For example, if you deduct all and the VAT return result is zero, the return type needs to be “N”.
  • Adjust Tax box 71 by subtracting the amount in tax box 112.
  • In case of corrections, adjust Tax box 111 with the newly calculated value of tax box 71 by subtracting the amount in tax box 112.

 

Tax Box 111 Calculation

The tax Agency has adjusted the calculation rules in case of rectification VAT return for tax box 111as follows:

  • Rectification VAT return = Yes
  • Tax Box [70] (Result to pay from previous returns of same year and period) is not zero.
  • Tax Box [71] (Final result ) is negative.
  • If Tax box[70] <= ABS(Tax box[71]),  then tax box [111] (Rectification amount other than discrepancy) = Tax box [70]
  • If Tax box[70] > ABS(Tax box[71]), then tax box [111] (Rectification amount other than discrepancy) = ABS(Tax box [71])

(Remark: ABS means absolute value, i.e.,  amount without sign)

TXT File Format Changes

The TXT file format has been restructured to accommodate the new fields.

  • The sequence of tax boxes [165] through [170] and [108] has been reordered within the file structure in records DP30301 and DP30303.
  • The CNAE values will change to 4 positions, instead of 3 in record structure DP30305.

 

Timeline

The regulation states in the final provision (disposicion final unica), that these changes will be applicable for the February reporting period:  "La presente orden…se aplicará por primera vez… a las autoliquidaciones del Impuesto sobre el Valor Añadido, modelos 303, 322 y 353 correspondientes al segundo trimestre o al mes de febrero de 2026".

Since the Tax Agency has activated the TXT format for January:

  • S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the change is expected to be available on March 1st;. Due to the time constraints, in case of S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition you can check the note 3716951 - TXT file for Legal form 303 for January 2026 rejected by the Tax Agency for additional support.
  • S/4HANA Cloud of Private Edition, the SAP Note 3717337 - ES_VAT_DCL_M303_NATIONAL - New format for reporting year 2026

Please stay tuned to the announcement Legal Form 303 (2026) in the Regulatory Change Manager.

Related Links

SAP remains committed to supporting customers through these regulatory changes, providing timely updates and comprehensive documentation to ensure smooth transitions and continued compliance. For technical questions or implementation assistance, please contact SAP Support or consult the relevant SAP Notes and documentation:

  • Document and Reporting Compliance in Public Cloud
  • Document and Reporting Compliance in Private Cloud

Disclaimer

This blog post is intended for informational purposes only and reflects a simplified configuration approach based on current legal and system understanding. It does not constitute legal or implementation advice. SAP system behavior may vary depending on version, scope, and customer-specific settings. For full compliance and implementation support, please consult your SAP implementation partner or legal advisor.

entry.title: Pregúntame lo que quieras con SAP Champions – Serie mensual (Sesión 2)
entry.content:
Hola querida SAP Community,

Mi nombre es María Antonieta López, SAP Champion de México.

Actualmente, trabajo como Directora de Programas SAP, enfocada en programas globales de transformación SAP, implementaciones de SAP S/4HANA, arquitectura empresarial, marcos de gobernanza e innovación digital dentro del ecosistema SAP.

Como parte de los SAP Champions que lideran la iniciativa “Ask Me Anything” junto con Sebastiano Marchesini,

Me complace informarles que yo estaré liderando la edición de marzo.🚀

Así que sí…

Pueden preguntarme. LO QUE SEA.

 

Después de más de 20 años viviendo dentro de transformaciones SAP, probablemente he visto lo bueno, lo malo… y esos momentos de “¿cómo sobrevivió este sistema al go-live?” 😅

 

Durante las próximas semanas estaré respondiendo cualquier pregunta que tengan — sin filtros — solo SAP en vivo y real.

💡Podemos explorar temas como:

⚙️Implementaciones de SAP S/4HANA y programas globales de transformación – Ejecución de implementaciones end-to-end de SAP S/4HANA y programas de transformación a gran escala, permitiendo a las organizaciones modernizar sus procesos de negocio centrales, estandarizar operaciones globales y adoptar capacidades digitales de nueva generación.

🏗️ Arquitectura Empresarial – Procesos, artefactos y estrategias para la arquitectura empresarial de SAP basada en TOGAF.

🧼 Clean Core en SAP Cloud ERP (Private & Public) — procesos de gestión de proyectos para implementar o crear extensiones o interfaces en tu proyecto.

(Hablando de BUILD para configuradores y su uso con Joule, APIs preconstruidas, APIs nuevas, y por supuesto nuestra plataforma como servicio SAP Integration Suite, todo dentro de nuestra querida SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)).

☁️ RISE o GROW con SAP + Gobernanza y metodología Clean Core

Desde SAP Activate hasta marcos como SAP Application Extension Methodology, ISA-M (Integration Solution Advisory Methodology), SAP Design Thinking, SAP Data Architecture Framework y, por qué no… incluso SAP Business AI Framework.

Puedes preguntar sobre qué artefactos usar, cómo ejecutar los procesos o por qué tengo que hacer esta tarea... Y te diré lo que pasará si te los saltas... Muahaha! …💀 así que mis historias de terror, como el invierno… están por llegar.

🏛️ Cómocrear un SAP Center of Excellence (CoE) o brindar servicios de SAP AM (Application Management) y realmente vivir felices para siempre con SAP usando Cloud ALM + Focused Build + Activate.

🌍 Globalización en SAP, ya sea de forma general o por país. ¡Larga vida a SAP Document & Reporting Compliance!

Y también a las extensiones DRC de nuestros partners SAP.

🧭 Roadmaps de soluciones y estrategia de transformación, etc

¿Y cualquier otra cosa? y ... ¡si no sé la respuesta! 👉 Prometo encontrar a alguien que sí la sepa… para ustedes.

 

Ya tenemos todo listo. 💬 Ahora es tu turno.

Deja tu pregunta en la sección de comentario👇 o puedes preguntar de forma anónima aquí 👉 AMA Champion Anonymously Questions o escaneando el QR Code 

 

📅Esta edición del AMA estará abierta para preguntas hasta el 27 de marzo de 2026.

Después de esa fecha, las preguntas seleccionadas serán respondidas en un video grabado que se publicará en el canal de YouTube de la SAP Community  👉 @SAPCommunity

También se publicará un blog resumen con las preguntas y respuestas para la comunidad.

 

Cuéntame…

🤓¿Qué tema de SAP te gustaría que exploremos juntos?

entry.title: Ask Me Anything with SAP Champions – Monthly Series (Session 2)
entry.content:
Hey dear SAP Community,

My name is Maria Antonieta Lopez,  I currently work as an SAP program director focusing on global SAP transformation programs, SAP S/4HANA implementations, enterprise architecture, governance frameworks, and digital innovation within the SAP ecosystem.

As an SAP Champion myself, I’ll be leading the March edition for the Ask Me Anything series, which we started together with my dear friend Sebastiano Marchesini.

So yes…

You can ask. Me. ANYTHING.

After 20+ years living inside SAP transformations, I’ve probably seen the good, the bad… and the “how did this system survive go-live?” moments. 😅

So over the next weeks I’ll be answering any question you have -- No filters - Just real SAP Live --.

💡We can explore topics like:

⚙️SAP S/4HANA implementations and global transformation programs. - How to successfully deliver end-to-end SAP S/4HANA implementations and large-scale transformation programs, enabling organizations to modernize their core business processes, standardize global operations, and adopt next-generation digital capabilities.

🏗️ SAP Enterprise Architecture. - Processes, artifacts, and strategies for TOGAF-based SAP enterprise architecture.

🧼 Clean Core in SAP Cloud ERP (Private & Public). - Project management processes for implementing or creating extensions or interfaces in your project. (BUILD for configurators and its use with Joule, pre-built APIs, newly developed APIs, and, of course, our platform-as-a-service, SAP Integration Suite, obviously within our beloved SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).

☁️ RISE or GROW with SAP + Clean Core Governance & Methodologies. - From SAP Activate  to frameworks such as SAP Application Extension Methodology, Integration Solution Advisory Methodology, SAP Design Thinking, SAP Data Architecture Framework, and why not… even the SAP Business AI Framework.

We can talk about which format to use, or the process for something, or even… why I have to do this task.... and I'll tell you what will happen if you don't...

Muahaha! …💀 soo Yes… my SAP horror stories - like winter - are coming…

🏛️ How to build an SAP Center of Excellence (CoE) or provide an SAP AM services and actually 💙 live happily ever after with SAP 💙 using Cloud ALM + Focused Build + Activate.

🌍Globalization in a nutshell or by country. To comply locally and succeed globally... Long live SAP Document & Reporting Compliance! And our SAP partners' DRC extensions.

🧭 Solution roadmaps, etc.

And... if I don't know the answer? 👉 I promise I'll find someone who does... for you.

 

We have everything ready...  💬 Now it’s your turn.

Drop your question in the comments section 👇or You can ask anonymously here 👉 AMA Champion Anonymously Questions  or Scanning this QR 

 

This AMA edition will be open for questions until March 27, 2026.

After that, selected questions will be answered in a recorded video published on the SAP Community YouTube channel  👉https://www.youtube.com/@SAPCommunity 
A blog recap summarizing the questions and answers will also be published for the community.

Cuentame…

🤓What SAP topic should we explore together? ESPAÑOL or ENGLISH

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Embedding Attachments in Belgium Peppol eInvoices
entry.content:
Introduction

Belgium's B2B eInvoicing mandate requires invoices to be transmitted via the Peppol network in UBL 2.1 format. Unlike some electronic document formats that support external document references, Peppol transmits the invoice as a single UBL XML file. This means there is no separate transmission channel for supporting documents — any attachment that needs to accompany the invoice must be embedded directly into the XML.

This blog post describes how to use the BAdI EDOC_ADAPTOR to read attachments from the GOS Attachment List of a billing document and embed them into the UBL XML, so they are transmitted together with the Peppol invoice or credit note.

Scope Note: This blog post covers the SAP S/4HANA on-premise implementation and S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition using BAdI EDOC_ADAPTOR. For SAP Public Edition, ABAP Environment, the equivalent BAdI is EDOC_ADAPTOR_CLOUD. The overall approach is similar, but backend APIs for attachment retrieval may differ and are not covered here.

How Attachments Are Embedded in UBL XML / BIS Billing 3.0

In UBL 2.1, supporting documents are referenced using the AdditionalDocumentReference element. For Peppol, the attachment content is Base64-encoded and placed directly inside the EmbeddedDocumentBinaryObject element. The following attributes are required:

  • mimeCode — the MIME type of the attached file (e.g., application/pdf, application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)
  • filename — the name of the attached file

The DocumentTypeCode element with value 130 is used to indicate that the document is a supporting reference and no attachment, in line with EN16931 / BG-24.

A typical XML structure looks as follows:

<cac:AdditionalDocumentReference>
  <cbc:ID>42010AEF83EE1FE187BE825366FAF87F</cbc:ID>
  <cac:Attachment>
    <cbc:EmbeddedDocumentBinaryObject
      mimeCode="application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet"
      filename="Sample Timesheet.xlsx">
      [Base64-encoded content]
    </cbc:EmbeddedDocumentBinaryObject>
  </cac:Attachment>
</cac:AdditionalDocumentReference>

Since Peppol does not support external URI references as a transmission mechanism, embedding is the only viable approach to include attachments in a Peppol invoice.

Prerequisites

Functional / Configuration Side

  • The Belgium EU eInvoice scenario is configured and active. You can verify this in the Manage Electronic Documents app, where the billing document should appear under the Belgium EU eInvoice process.
  • The attachment has been uploaded to the GOS Attachment List of the relevant billing document (via Services for Object → Create → Create Attachment).
  • Keep individual attachments below 3 MB. While Peppol BIS Billing 3.0 does not impose a strict size limit, large embedded files can cause transmission issues. As a general guideline, keep the total embedded attachment size well below 3 MB.

ABAP / Developer Side

  • BAdI EDOC_ADAPTOR is available in the system.
  • A custom BAdI implementation will be created as described in the next section.
  • The relevant filter values are:
    • COUNTRY = BE
    • GENERIC_FILTER = (empty)

Implementation: BAdI EDOC_ADAPTOR

Create the BAdI Implementation

  1. Go to transaction SE18 and search for BAdI EDOC_ADAPTOR.
  2. Create a new implementation , e.g. ZEI_EDOC_ADAPTOR_BE.
  3. Set the filter values as follows:

Filter Value

COUNTRYBE
GENERIC_FILTER(leave empty)
  1. Navigate to method IF_EDOC_ADAPTOR~SET_OUTPUT_DATA and add the implementation described in the following sections.
  2. Activate the BAdI implementation.

Check Source Type and Retrieve the Billing Document

The first step inside the method is to verify that the source document is an SD billing document and to retrieve the billing document number. If the source type is not SD_INVOICE or the billing document number is initial, the method exits without further processing.

DATA: ls_sd_src_data TYPE edoc_src_data_sd_invoice.

" Only process SD Invoice source documents
IF io_source->mv_source_type = 'SD_INVOICE'.

  " Retrieve SD source data including the billing document header
  io_source->get_data( IMPORTING es_data = ls_sd_src_data ).

  " Only proceed if a valid billing document number is available
  IF ls_sd_src_data-document_header-vbeln IS NOT INITIAL.

    " ... (attachment retrieval and embedding — see below)

  ENDIF.

ENDIF.

Retrieve Attachments via cl_odata_cv_attachment_api

Attachments are retrieved using the cl_odata_cv_attachment_api class. This API reads all attachments stored in the GOS Attachment List for a given business object. In this case, the object type is VBRK (billing document header) and the object key is the billing document number.

DATA: lo_attachment_api TYPE REF TO cl_odata_cv_attachment_api,
      lt_attachments    TYPE if_odata_cv_attachment_api=>ty_t_attachment_content,
      ls_attachment     TYPE if_odata_cv_attachment_api=>ty_s_attachment_content.

" Get singleton instance of the attachment API
lo_attachment_api = cl_odata_cv_attachment_api=>get_instance( ).

" Retrieve all attachments for the billing document
TRY.
    lo_attachment_api->if_odata_cv_attachment_api~retrieve_attachments_content(
      EXPORTING
        iv_objecttype          = 'VBRK'
        iv_objectkey           = CONV objky( ls_sd_src_data-document_header-vbeln )
      IMPORTING
        et_attachment_contents = lt_attachments
    ).
  CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(lx_root).
    MESSAGE lx_root->get_text( ) TYPE 'I'.
    RETURN.
ENDTRY.

The result is an internal table lt_attachments containing one entry per attachment, including the file content, filename, and MIME type.

Filter Attachments (Optional)

By default, all attachments stored in the GOS Attachment List are retrieved and will be embedded into the XML. If you need to restrict which attachments are included — for example, to include only PDF files — you can filter the internal table before processing:

" Example: only include PDF attachments
DELETE lt_attachments WHERE mimetype NE 'application/pdf'.

Size Reminder: Before embedding, consider the total size of all attachments. As noted in the prerequisites, keep the combined embedded content below 3 MB to avoid potential transmission issues.

Embed Attachments into the UBL XML

The method handles two document types: invoices (UBL_INVOICE_TRANSM) and credit notes (UBL_CREDIT_NOTE_TRANSM). The correct path is determined by checking iv_interface_id. For each attachment, the following fields are mapped into the AdditionalDocumentReference structure of the UBL XML:

Attachment API Field UBL XML Element

file_idID
filenameDocumentDescription + filename attribute
mimetypemimeCode attribute
attachment_contentEmbeddedDocumentBinaryObject content

The DocumentTypeCode is set to 130 to indicate a document reference per EN16931 / BG-24.

Invoice path:

FIELD-SYMBOLS:
  <ls_out_invoice> TYPE edo_ubl_standard_business_doc2,
  <fs_attachment>  TYPE edo_ubl_document_reference.

          IF iv_interface_id = 'UBL_INVOICE_TRANSM'.
            ASSIGN cs_output_data TO <ls_out_struct_invoice>.
            IF <ls_out_struct_invoice> IS NOT ASSIGNED.
              RETURN.
            ENDIF.

            " Loop over all retrieved attachments and add them to the invoice
            LOOP AT lt_attachments INTO ls_attachment.
              APPEND INITIAL LINE TO <ls_out_struct_invoice>-invoice-additional_document_reference
                     ASSIGNING <fs_attachment>.

              " Populate attachment fields
              <fs_attachment>-id-base-base-content = ls_attachment-file_id.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-content = ls_attachment-attachment_content.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-filename = ls_attachment-filename.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-mime_code = ls_attachment-mimetype.

              " Optional: Set document type code (e.g., '130' for attachment)
*              <fs_attachment>-document_type_code-base-base-content = '130'.
            ENDLOOP.

Credit Note path:

FIELD-SYMBOLS:
  <ls_out_crenote> TYPE edo_ubl_standard_business_doc5,
  <fs_attachment>  TYPE edo_ubl_document_reference.
          ELSEIF iv_interface_id = 'UBL_CREDIT_NOTE_TRANSM'.
            ASSIGN cs_output_data TO <ls_out_struct_crenote>.
            IF <ls_out_struct_crenote> IS NOT ASSIGNED.
              RETURN.
            ENDIF.

            " Loop over all retrieved attachments and add them to the credit note
            LOOP AT lt_attachments INTO ls_attachment.
              APPEND INITIAL LINE TO <ls_out_struct_crenote>-credit_note-additional_document_reference
                     ASSIGNING <fs_attachment>.

              " Populate attachment fields
              <fs_attachment>-id-base-base-content = ls_attachment-file_id.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-content = ls_attachment-attachment_content.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-filename = ls_attachment-filename.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-mime_code = ls_attachment-mimetype.

              " Optional: Set document type code
*              <fs_attachment>-document_type_code-base-base-content = '130'.
            ENDLOOP.

          ENDIF.

Complete Source Code

METHOD if_edoc_adaptor~set_output_data.
      " ========================================================================
      " Retrieve and embed attachments for SD Invoice / Credit Note
      " Only executed when processing an SD billing document
      " ========================================================================

      DATA: ls_sd_src_data TYPE edoc_src_data_sd_invoice.

      " Check if this is an SD Invoice source
      IF io_source->mv_source_type = 'SD_INVOICE'.

        " Get the SD source data (billing document header and items)
        io_source->get_data( IMPORTING es_data = ls_sd_src_data ).

        " Only proceed if we have a valid billing document number
        IF ls_sd_src_data-document_header-vbeln IS NOT INITIAL.

          " ====================================================================
          " Step 1: Retrieve attachment IDs and content from GOS
          " ====================================================================
          DATA: lo_attachment_api TYPE REF TO cl_odata_cv_attachment_api,
                lt_attachments    TYPE if_odata_cv_attachment_api=>ty_t_attachment_content,
                ls_attachment     TYPE if_odata_cv_attachment_api=>ty_s_attachment_content.

          " Get singleton instance of the attachment API
          lo_attachment_api = cl_odata_cv_attachment_api=>get_instance( ).

          " Retrieve all attachments for the billing document
          " Object type = 'VBRK', Object key = billing document number (10 chars)
          TRY.
              lo_attachment_api->if_odata_cv_attachment_api~retrieve_attachments_content(
                EXPORTING
                  iv_objecttype  = 'VBRK'
                  iv_objectkey   = CONV objky( ls_sd_src_data-document_header-vbeln )
                IMPORTING
                  et_attachment_contents = lt_attachments
              ).
            CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(lx_root).
              " Log or handle error if attachment retrieval fails
              MESSAGE lx_root->get_text( ) TYPE 'I'.
              RETURN.
          ENDTRY.

          " ====================================================================
          " Step 2: Filter attachments (optional)
          " ====================================================================
          " TODO: Add your attachment filter logic here if needed.
          " For example, you may want to exclude certain file types or only
          " include attachments with a specific document type code.
          " Example:
          "   DELETE lt_attachments WHERE mime_type NE 'application/pdf'.
          " ====================================================================

          " ====================================================================
          " Step 3: Embed attachments into the UBL XML structure
          " ====================================================================
          FIELD-SYMBOLS:
            <ls_out_struct_invoice> TYPE edo_ubl_standard_business_doc2,
            <ls_out_struct_crenote> TYPE edo_ubl_standard_business_doc5,
            <fs_attachment>         TYPE edo_ubl_document_reference.

          " Determine if this is an Invoice or Credit Note
          IF iv_interface_id = 'UBL_INVOICE_TRANSM'.
            ASSIGN cs_output_data TO <ls_out_struct_invoice>.
            IF <ls_out_struct_invoice> IS NOT ASSIGNED.
              RETURN.
            ENDIF.

            " Loop over all retrieved attachments and add them to the invoice
            LOOP AT lt_attachments INTO ls_attachment.
              APPEND INITIAL LINE TO <ls_out_struct_invoice>-invoice-additional_document_reference
                     ASSIGNING <fs_attachment>.

              " Populate attachment fields
              <fs_attachment>-id-base-base-content = ls_attachment-file_id.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-content = ls_attachment-attachment_content.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-filename = ls_attachment-filename.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-mime_code = ls_attachment-mimetype.

              " Optional: Set document type code rather than attachment
*              <fs_attachment>-document_type_code-base-base-content = '130'.
            ENDLOOP.

          ELSEIF iv_interface_id = 'UBL_CREDIT_NOTE_TRANSM'.
            ASSIGN cs_output_data TO <ls_out_struct_crenote>.
            IF <ls_out_struct_crenote> IS NOT ASSIGNED.
              RETURN.
            ENDIF.

            " Loop over all retrieved attachments and add them to the credit note
            LOOP AT lt_attachments INTO ls_attachment.
              APPEND INITIAL LINE TO <ls_out_struct_crenote>-credit_note-additional_document_reference
                     ASSIGNING <fs_attachment>.

              " Populate attachment fields
              <fs_attachment>-id-base-base-content = ls_attachment-file_id.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-content = ls_attachment-attachment_content.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-filename = ls_attachment-filename.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-mime_code = ls_attachment-mimetype.

              " Optional: Set document type code rather than attachment
*              <fs_attachment>-document_type_code-base-base-content = '130'.
            ENDLOOP.

          ENDIF.

        ENDIF. " vbeln is not initial

      ENDIF. " source_type = SD_INVOICE

      " ========================================================================
      " Continue with any other custom logic below...
      " ======================================================================== 
 ENDMETHOD.

Result and Verification

Once the BAdI implementation is active, you can verify the end-to-end flow using the following steps.

Step 1: Add an Attachment to the Billing Document

Open the billing document in change mode (e.g., via VF02 or the Billing Documents app). Navigate to Services for Object → Create → Create Attachment and upload your file. The attachment will appear in the GOS Attachment List.

Step 2: Verify the Document in Manage Electronic Documents

Open the Manage Electronic Documents app and locate the billing document. The document should appear under the Belgium EU eInvoice process with status Created (In Process).

Step 3: Preview the XML

Select the document and choose Display/Preview XML. In the XML output, locate the AdditionalDocumentReference block. You should see:

  • The ID populated with the GOS file ID
  • The DocumentDescription containing the filename
  • The EmbeddedDocumentBinaryObject element with the correct mimeCode, filename, and Base64-encoded file content

 

Summary

In this blog post, we covered how to embed GOS attachments into a Belgium Peppol eInvoice XML using BAdI EDOC_ADAPTOR on SAP S/4HANA on-premise.

Key takeaways:

  • Peppol transmits invoices as a single UBL XML file — embedding is the only option for including attachments
  • Attachments can be retrieved from the GOS Attachment List using cl_odata_cv_attachment_api
  • The sample implementation handles both invoices (UBL_INVOICE_TRANSM) and credit notes (UBL_CREDIT_NOTE_TRANSM) via the iv_interface_id parameter
  • BAdI filter: COUNTRY = BE, GENERIC_FILTER = (empty)
  • Keep total embedded attachment size below 3 MB to avoid transmission issues
  • For SAP S/4HANA Cloud, ABAP Environment, use EDOC_ADAPTOR_CLOUD — backend APIs for attachment retrieval may differ

entry.title: Price Amount Recalculation in Peppol E-Invoicing for SAP
entry.content:
Introduction

In the world of electronic invoicing, especially within the Peppol network, precision is paramount. A single misplaced decimal can lead to rejected invoices, delayed payments, and frustrated business partners. One of the frequently discussed topics is the calculation of the PriceAmount field in UBL invoices. This blog post aims to shed light on why SAP's standard solution for Document and Reporting Compliance (e-invoicing) behaves the way it does, and how you can tailor it to your specific business needs.

Scope Note: This blog post covers the SAP S/4HANA on-premise implementation and S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition using BAdI EDOC_ADAPTOR, and assuming the latest version as per 2783795 - Electronic Document Processing for Peppol Countries/Regions: Implementation Overview - SAP for Me is implemented. 
This blog post refers only to eDocuments which were created out of Sales & Distribution Billing documents, as those allow price conditions as allowances or surcharges.
For SAP Public Edition, ABAP Environment, the equivalent BAdI is EDOC_ADAPTOR_CLOUD. The overall approach is similar, but backend APIs to format the amounts in the XML may differ and are not covered here.

As this behavior is cross-country enabled and as the mapping for CrossIndustryInvoice for Germany is implemented on top of the Peppol mapping, this is also valid for Germany / ZUGFeRD process as outlined here.

What is the Issue? The Peppol Rule PEPPOL-EN16931-R120

At the heart of the matter lies a critical Peppol validation rule: PEPPOL-EN16931-R120. This rule mandates that the Invoice line net amount must be consistent with the quantity and price. In simple terms, the following equation must hold true (within a defined rounding tolerance):

LineExtensionAmount = InvoicedQuantity * (PriceAmount / BaseQuantity)

If an invoice line includes discounts or surcharges (AllowanceCharge), the formula expands:

LineExtensionAmount = (InvoicedQuantity * (PriceAmount / BaseQuantity)) - Allowances + Charges

If this mathematical check fails, the Peppol Access Point will reject the invoice. The ultimate goal of the SAP standard solution is to ensure every generated invoice is compliant and successfully transmitted through the network.

Why SAP Cannot Deliver the Perfect Calculation in Every Case

The challenge arises because different businesses represent prices and discounts in different ways. SAP's e-invoicing solution must balance flexibility with the strict need for compliance.

SAP's standard mapping tries to generate a valid invoice that will be accepted by the Peppol Network. It does this by recalculating the PriceAmount if it detects a potential violation of the R120 rule. This often happens in two common scenarios:

  1. Hidden Discounts/Surcharges: Many businesses prefer not to show item-level discounts or surcharges separately in the final XML, at least not all of them. At the same time, they want the PriceAmount to reflect the net price. If the discount/surcharge conditions are not mapped to the <cac:AllowanceCharge> element in the XML, the standard PriceAmount (often derived from a gross price condition) will not match the final LineExtensionAmount. SAP's logic detects this and recalculates the PriceAmount to absorb these "hidden" values.

  2. High-Precision Prices: For low-priced items sold in bulk, kWh sold in Utilities, or items with prices derived from complex calculations, the calculated unit price can have many decimal places (e.g., €7.39933 per piece). The UBL standard allows for this, but historically, currency fields were often handled with only two decimal places. SAP's standard logic scales the price, but keeps two digits, avoiding rounding errors that would violate rule PEPPOL-EN16931-R120. So instead of showing 7.39933 / 1 piece, it might show 7399.33 / 1000 pieces. While mathematically identical, this can be confusing for human readers of the invoice.

This scaling logic is what customers may find "weird". However, it's a safe-by-default mechanism to guarantee compliance.

Tailoring the Calculation with a BAdI Implementation

The good news is that you have full control. Using the Business Add-In (BAdI) EDOC_ADAPTOR, you can implement your own logic to override the standard scaling behavior.

The best place to do this is in the BAdI method IF_EDOC_ADAPTOR~SET_OUTPUT_DATA. This method is called after SAP has performed its standard mapping, giving you the final data structure just before it's converted to XML.

Here is a sample implementation that rescales the price based on a simple rule: if the invoiced quantity is less than 1000, use a BaseQuantity of 1; otherwise, use 1000. This provides a more predictable and readable output, and can be fine-tuned to your needs.

CLASS zcl_edoc_adaptor_scaling IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD if_edoc_adaptor~set_output_data.
    " This BAdI implementation rescales the PriceAmount and BaseQuantity
    " according to a custom logic, overriding the standard SAP scaling.

    FIELD-SYMBOLS:
      <fs_invoice_data>   TYPE any,
      <lt_invoice_line>   TYPE STANDARD TABLE,
      <fs_line>           TYPE any,
      <fs_price>          TYPE any,
      <fs_price_amount>   TYPE any,
      <fs_base_quantity>  TYPE any,
      <fs_inv_quantity>   TYPE any.

    DATA:
      lv_current_price     TYPE p LENGTH 16 DECIMALS 10,
      lv_current_base_qty  TYPE p LENGTH 16 DECIMALS 10,
      lv_invoiced_quantity TYPE fklmg,
      lv_unit_price        TYPE p LENGTH 16 DECIMALS 10,
      lv_new_base_quantity TYPE p LENGTH 16 DECIMALS 10,
      lv_new_price_amount  TYPE p LENGTH 16 DECIMALS 10.

    IF iv_interface_id CS 'UBL_INVOICE_'.
      ASSIGN cs_output_data TO <fs_invoice_data>.
      IF <fs_invoice_data> IS NOT ASSIGNED. RETURN. ENDIF.

      ASSIGN COMPONENT 'INVOICE-INVOICE_LINE' OF STRUCTURE <fs_invoice_data> TO <lt_invoice_line>.
      IF <lt_invoice_line> IS NOT ASSIGNED. RETURN. ENDIF.

      LOOP AT <lt_invoice_line> ASSIGNING <fs_line>.
        " Get current Price, BaseQuantity, and InvoicedQuantity
        ASSIGN COMPONENT 'PRICE' OF STRUCTURE <fs_line> TO <fs_price>.
        ASSIGN COMPONENT 'INVOICED_QUANTITY-BASE-BASE-CONTENT' OF STRUCTURE <fs_line> TO <fs_inv_quantity>.
        IF <fs_price> IS NOT ASSIGNED OR <fs_inv_quantity> IS NOT ASSIGNED.
          CONTINUE.
        ENDIF.

        ASSIGN COMPONENT 'PRICE_AMOUNT-BASE-CONTENT' OF STRUCTURE <fs_price> TO <fs_price_amount>.
        ASSIGN COMPONENT 'BASE_QUANTITY-BASE-BASE-CONTENT' OF STRUCTURE <fs_price> TO <fs_base_quantity>.

        IF <fs_price_amount> IS NOT ASSIGNED OR <fs_base_quantity> IS NOT ASSIGNED.
          CONTINUE.
        ENDIF.
        
        lv_current_price = <fs_price_amount>.
        lv_current_base_qty = <fs_base_quantity>.
        lv_invoiced_quantity = <fs_inv_quantity>.

        IF lv_current_base_qty = 0.
          CONTINUE.
        ENDIF.

        " 1. Calculate the true unit price from the existing values
        lv_unit_price = lv_current_price / lv_current_base_qty.

        " 2. *** CUSTOMER-SPECIFIC SCALING LOGIC STARTS HERE ***
        IF lv_invoiced_quantity < 1000.
          lv_new_base_quantity = 1.
        ELSE.
          lv_new_base_quantity = 1000.
        ENDIF.
        " *** CUSTOMER-SPECIFIC SCALING LOGIC ENDS HERE ***

        " 3. Calculate the new PriceAmount based on the new BaseQuantity
        lv_new_price_amount = lv_unit_price * lv_new_base_quantity.

        " 4. Write the new values back and format for XML output
        <fs_price_amount>  = lv_new_price_amount.
        <fs_base_quantity> = lv_new_base_quantity.

        cl_edoc_map_aif=>format_negative_amount( CHANGING cv_amount = <fs_price_amount> ).
        cl_edoc_map_aif=>format_negative_amount( CHANGING cv_amount = <fs_base_quantity> ).

      ENDLOOP.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

Summary

Navigating Peppol compliance requires a careful balance between standard processes and business-specific needs. While SAP's default behavior for PriceAmount recalculation prioritizes compliance with rule PEPPOL-EN16931-R120 to ensure your invoices are accepted, it may not always align with your desired representation.

Key Takeaways

  • Compliance is King: The primary reason for the recalculation is to comply with PEPPOL-EN16931-R120 and prevent invoice rejection.
  • You Have Choices: You can configure how discounts and surcharges appear. Either show them separately in <cac:AllowanceCharge> elements or absorb them into a net PriceAmount. The latter often triggers the recalculation.
  • You Have Control: The EDOC_ADAPTOR BAdI provides a powerful, upgrade-safe way to implement your own pricing representation logic, overriding the default scaling to produce invoices that are both compliant and easy for your customers to understand.
